summary: Patrick McKenzie shares a fan-created tool that reorganizes his blog into chronological order. A reader named Rizal built a 'MicroISV on a Shoestring Reader' mashup that displays the blog from oldest to newest, solving the problem that blog software defaults to reverse-chronological order. McKenzie appreciates this because he often directs people to read his blog from the beginning to understand his uISV journey.
key_points: McKenzie's blog serves as the narrative of how his micro-ISV came about, and he regularly directs people to read it from the start
Blog software's default reverse-chronological ordering is a poor fit for following a business journey over time
A community member independently built a tool to solve this UX problem, demonstrating early reader engagement
money_quotes: When folks ask me "So how exactly did your uISV come about?", I generally tell them to go to the start of the blog and start reading.
key_insight: A blog documenting a micro-ISV journey is best read chronologically, but blog software defaults work against this, creating an opportunity for reader-built tools.
